goal: Let security teams run controlled SMS phishing simulations, measure user behavior per graduated risk signals, and trigger targeted remediation from the same risk-management workflows already used for email phishing and training.
user_segments: people manager (receives alerts on repeat failures)
end user / employee (receives simulation and coaching)
customer success / Dune Studio (template and reporting reuse)
success_metrics: Admin creates and launches a campaign in under 10 minutes
95%+ of test sends complete successfully in supported regions
User risk events appear within 60 seconds of click/submission
Remediation triggers fire within 60 seconds for event-based rules
50% of pilot customers launch a second smishing campaign within 60 days
Zero collection of real credentials, OTPs, MFA codes, or sensitive data
Click rate decreases across repeated campaigns for the same user cohort
Report rate increases across repeated campaigns
Repeat failure rate decreases for users receiving remediation
constraints: v1 scope: SMS-only; US-first or limited supported-region beta
Template-based personalization only in v1 (approved library + light customization)
No WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, iMessage, vishing, malware simulation, or two-way chatbot in v1
No collection of real passwords, OTPs, MFA codes, SSNs, or payment data at any time
Customer must confirm lawful basis and employee-notice process — Dune does not own underlying TCPA/GDPR compliance
Phone number storage must use hashing or tokenization (phone_hash in data model)
Standard SMS limited to 160 characters (GSM-7) or 70 characters (Unicode)
No existing phone number data model in Dune — net-new infrastructure
Carrier registration required for sending infrastructure
Architecture must be extensible to WhatsApp, Signal, Telegram, QR-code lures, and vishing from day one
assumptions: Dune currently has no employee phone number storage — this is net-new infrastructure
v1 ships template-based; AI personalization engine adaptation is post-v1
Phone number sourcing approach (HRIS, CSV, manual) is a Phase 0 discovery item — not decided at PRD writing
Sending provider (Twilio, AWS SNS, etc.) is a Phase 0 decision
Reporting behavior (user forwarding suspicious SMS) is a Phase 0 discovery item for v1 scope decision
Approval workflows for high-sensitivity templates may be needed — not yet decided for v1
gaps: Phone number sourcing strategy undefined — Phase 0 customer interviews required
Exact risk-weighting values for email vs. smishing signals not defined
Opt-out and consent handling by customer/region/campaign type not specified
Approval workflow scope for high-sensitivity templates not decided
Template customer-editability in v1 (full custom vs. token-substitution only) not decided
Template guardrails to prevent harmful impersonation not designed
Sender identity specifics (short code vs. 10DLC) not decided
Link scanner false positive handling not addressed in PRD
STOP reply permanence (campaign-scoped vs. permanent exclusion) not addressed
edge_cases: Group members with no phone number on file — partial campaign delivery
International phone numbers unsupported in v1
Employee replies STOP — must exclude from current and future sends (permanence TBD)
Link scanner auto-follows tracking URL — false positive click event
Phone number data goes stale mid-campaign (employee off-boards)
Unicode content drops character limit to 70
Admin targets group where large fraction has no phone number
Admin selects credential harvest template — must show safety warning callout with acknowledgment; Step 3 renders simulated login form config summary
Admin selects MFA harvest template — same warning; Step 3 renders simulated verification form config summary
Simulated credential form submitted — event recorded as smishing_credential_submitted; no data stored; debrief State B served
Simulated MFA form submitted — event recorded as smishing_mfa_submitted; no data stored; debrief State C served
Password manager autofills simulated credential form — must NOT trigger smishing_credential_submitted; only submit button click triggers event
Employee navigates back to simulated form after debrief — form page shows 'This simulation has ended' state; form disabled; event not re-recorded
Test-send recipient receives message — should be labeled as test, not simulation
Campaign cloned from email phishing concept — field mapping and template adaptation needed
Repeat offender across multiple campaigns — escalating risk signal must fire correctly

critical_questions.question: Where do customer phone numbers live today in Dune, and what permissions exist around them? Do customers expect smishing to use personal or work-issued numbers?
What SMS sending provider should Dune use, and which countries should v1 support?
How should opt-out and employee consent be handled by customer, region, and campaign type?
What is the exact risk-weighting difference between email-phishing failures and smishing failures?
Is user reporting (forwarding suspicious SMS) in scope for v1, or v1 tracks only click/submission?
Are approval workflows required for high-sensitivity templates (credential-harvest, executive impersonation)?
Are smishing templates customer-editable in v1, or approved-library only during beta?
How will Dune prevent customers from sending messages that look like real emergencies, legal notices, or harmful impersonations?
How will link scanner false positives be detected and excluded from click metrics and risk scores?
When an employee replies STOP, is the exclusion permanent across all future campaigns or scoped to the current campaign?

design_risks: Phone coverage cold-start: feature is immediately limited by phone number data quality — onboarding path for phone data must be visible before first campaign creation

Credential and MFA harvest trust risk: if the simulated form page does not immediately show the debrief after submission, employees may believe they submitted real data — the form must redirect to debrief without any intermediate state that could be confused for a real service response
Approval workflow gap: if high-sensitivity templates require approval and the approval state is not designed, campaigns will be blocked with no visible resolution path
Link scanner false positives inflate metrics and corrupt risk scores — mechanism for filtering is not yet defined

Graduated risk scoring complexity: five distinct event types (delivered, clicked, submitted, reported, training_completed) require a reporting UI that shows each separately — collapsing them into a single click metric loses the PRD's intended behavioral nuance
teaching_notes: PRD data model: campaign type=smishing; simulation_type field: link_only | credential_harvest | mfa_harvest; recipient event stores phone_hash not raw phone number; events per recipient: smishing_link_clicked, smishing_credential_submitted (credential only), smishing_mfa_submitted (MFA only), smishing_reported, smishing_training_assigned, smishing_training_completed

Risk scoring is graduated: delivered=no impact, clicked=moderate negative, credential_submitted=high negative, mfa_submitted=high negative (potentially same weight as credential, to be confirmed), reported=positive, training_completed=positive recovery, repeated failure=escalating

Credential/MFA form safety model: three-layer enforcement — (1) client: submit handler fires event + redirect, no data serialization; (2) server: endpoint accepts no payload beyond campaign token; (3) admin UI: non-dismissible safety lock callout in template form
Debrief content structure from PRD §13: Hook → Red flags (from specific message) → Correct behavior → Micro-commitment
v1 entry point per PRD: Simulations → Smishing sub-nav, not a channel selector at wizard start
Compliance model: customer confirms lawful basis at campaign creation — Dune provides the acknowledgment step, customer owns the underlying legal basis
Architecture must anticipate WhatsApp/Signal/Telegram/QR/vishing from day one even though v1 ships SMS-only
